judgmentZh: 萃：亨。王假有庙，利见大人，亨，利贞。用大牲吉，利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Gathering Together. Success. The king approaches his temple. It furthers one to see the great man. This brings success. Perseverance furthers. To bring great offerings creates good fortune. It furthers one to undertake something.
imageZh: 泽上于地，萃；君子以除戎器，戒不虞。
imageEn: Over the earth, the lake: the image of Gathering Together. Thus the superior man renews his weapons in order to meet the unforeseen.
interpretationZh: 萃卦象征聚集，泽水汇聚于地上。人心聚合，宜以诚敬之心祭祀，团结众人共成大事。
interpretationEn: Cui symbolizes gathering and assembly. The lake collects over the earth. Unite people with sincerity and reverence to accomplish great things.

lines.textZh: 初六：有孚不终，乃乱乃萃，若号，一握为笑，勿恤，往无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: If you are sincere, but not to the end, there will sometimes be confusion, sometimes gathering. If you call out, then after one grasp of the hand you can laugh again. Regret not. Going is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 诚信不能坚持到底，时聚时散。呼唤同伴，握手言欢，前往无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Sincerity wavers, causing confusion. Call out for help, join hands, and move forward without blame.
lines.detailZh: 初六处萃之始，志未定而群情已动，故曰“有孚不终，乃乱乃萃”。起初虽有诚信，却容易因立场不稳、响应纷杂而陷入混乱。但爻辞接着说“若号一握为笑，勿恤，往无咎”，表示只要肯真诚呼求、重新收拢人心，纷扰仍可化解。此爻提醒人在刚进入集体关系时，不可随波逐流，也不可因一时尴尬而退缩，应主动表达、寻求同道，便能免咎。
lines.detailEn: The first line stands at the beginning of Gathering, where commitment is still unstable and collective emotion is easily stirred. Hence it says there is trust, but not to the end, so there is disorder and then gathering. The line shows an initial convergence that lacks steadiness. Yet it adds that if one cries out and then shares a laugh in close contact, there is no need for worry; going forward brings no blame. This means confusion at the outset can be corrected through honest communication and renewed human connection. The lesson is not to drift passively within a group, nor to withdraw because of embarrassment. Recenter, reach out, and regroup.

lines.textZh: 六二：引吉，无咎，孚乃利用禴。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Letting oneself be drawn brings good fortune and remains blameless. If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering.
lines.interpretationZh: 被引导而聚合则吉，无咎。心怀诚信，即使简单的祭祀也有利。
lines.interpretationEn: Being drawn into the gathering brings fortune. With sincerity, even a small offering furthers.
lines.detailZh: 六二柔顺中正，上应九五，是萃卦中最能以诚相感的一爻。“引吉，无咎”，说明在正确引导之下，聚合可以自然成形；“孚乃利用禴”，则强调不必铺张，真诚即可通神达意。此爻象征在群体中扮演温和而可靠的连接者：不争主位，却能承上启下。现实中代表值得信赖的协调、引荐、撮合与辅助力量，贵在朴实诚恳。
lines.detailEn: The second line is yielding, central, and correct, responding upward to the fifth line. It is one of the most harmonious lines in the hexagram. “Leading brings good fortune, no blame” suggests that proper guidance allows gathering to form naturally. “Sincerity then benefits from the simple offering” teaches that extravagance is unnecessary when inner truth is present. This line represents the trustworthy mediator within a group—someone who does not compete for dominance but links people effectively. In practical terms, it favors coordination, recommendation, facilitation, and modest sincerity.

lines.textZh: 六三：萃如嗟如，无攸利，往无咎，小吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Gathering together amid sighs. Nothing that would further. Going is without blame. Slight humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 聚合中叹息不已，无所利益。前往虽无咎，但有小憾。
lines.interpretationEn: Gathering with sighs. No advantage here. Going forward is blameless but slightly humiliating.
lines.detailZh: 六三居下卦之上，想参与聚集却不得其位，故有“萃如嗟如”之叹。此时内心焦灼、外缘不足，若强求利益，往往“无攸利”。不过爻辞又言“往无咎，小吝”，表示虽然处境尴尬，只要不困于自怨，仍可向前寻求正当归附，不至于犯大错。此爻提示：当自己在圈层中位置不稳时，不必过度感叹得失，暂时的小委屈，往往是重新定位前的必经阶段。
lines.detailEn: The third line occupies an uneasy place. It wants to participate in the gathering but lacks proper standing, so it sighs in frustration: “Gathering with lament.” Under such conditions, there is little advantage in pursuing gain. Still, the line says that going forward brings no blame, though there is slight regret. The meaning is that even when one feels excluded or poorly placed, one should not become trapped in complaint. By moving toward a rightful connection rather than chasing immediate benefit, one avoids serious error. It is a line of awkward belonging, temporary discomfort, and necessary repositioning.

lines.textZh: 九四：大吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Great good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 大吉大利，没有过失。以公心聚众，自然顺遂。
lines.interpretationEn: Great good fortune and no blame. Gathering others with a public spirit brings success.
lines.detailZh: 九四以阳居近君之位，能够承接众情、辅佐中心，故曰“大吉，无咎”。此爻之吉，在于它既有行动力，又不越九五之尊，懂得在关键位置发挥组织与推动作用。若把萃比作大型组织，九四便像核心骨干、统筹负责人或能臣干将。它提醒人们：在群体凝聚之时，次核心位置尤为重要，既要承担压力，也要守住分寸，辅主成事而不争功。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line is strong and close to the ruler, able to receive the sentiments of the many and support the central authority. Therefore it says: “Great good fortune, no blame.” Its excellence lies in active capability joined with restraint. It does important work without overstepping the fifth line. In organizational terms, this is the key lieutenant, operations leader, or trusted executor who helps transform collective potential into effective structure. The line teaches that secondary leadership is crucial in times of gathering: one must carry responsibility, maintain proportion, and serve the whole rather than compete for prominence.

lines.textZh: 九五：萃有位，无咎。匪孚，元永贞，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: If in gathering together one has position, this is without blame. If there are some who are not yet sincerely in the work, sublime and enduring perseverance is needed. Then remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 居位而聚众，无咎。若有人尚未诚服，需以恒久正道感化，悔恨自消。
lines.interpretationEn: Gathering from a position of authority, no blame. Win over doubters through enduring perseverance and remorse vanishes.
lines.detailZh: 九五居中得正，为全卦之主，故曰“萃有位，无咎”。真正的聚合，必须有其位、有其主、有其名分。然而爻辞又说“匪孚，元永贞，悔亡”，说明即便身居中心，也未必立刻得到人人信服，唯有以深厚德行、长久正道坚持下去，疑虑才会渐消，悔亦可亡。这是领导者之爻：位置给了你合法性，但真正赢得众心，还要靠时间、诚信与持续稳定。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line is central, correct, and the ruler of the hexagram, so it says: “Gathering has position, no blame.” True gathering requires recognized place, authority, and legitimacy. Yet the line continues: “If there is lack of trust, great and enduring correctness brings remorse to an end.” This means that formal position alone does not guarantee inner loyalty from others. Even a rightful leader may face doubt at first. Lasting integrity, consistency, and moral steadiness are what gradually dissolve mistrust. This is the line of leadership itself: authority may establish order, but only virtue sustains collective faith.

lines.textZh: 上六：赍咨涕洟，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Lamenting and sighing, floods of tears. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 叹息流泪，悲伤不已，但并无过失。真情流露，终能感动人心。
lines.interpretationEn: Lamenting with floods of tears. No blame. Genuine emotion ultimately moves others.
lines.detailZh: 上六居萃之终，情感过盛而失其依，故曰“赍咨涕洟，无咎”。这里有叹息、哭泣、忧惧之象，仿佛聚到最后反觉孤单或担心失散。虽显狼狈，却“无咎”，因为真情流露本身并非过错，反而说明此人仍重视关系、未至麻木。此爻告诫人们，在聚散边缘，情绪容易放大，不妨坦然承认不安，但不要让哀感取代判断。能从情绪中回到正位，聚散皆可安然。
lines.detailEn: The top line stands at the end of Gathering, where emotion overflows and secure attachment weakens. Thus it speaks of sighing, tears, and running nose, yet says there is no blame. This is an image of vulnerability at the edge of collective life—after the height of togetherness, one may fear separation, irrelevance, or loss. Though the display seems undignified, it is not blameworthy, because genuine feeling is better than numbness or false composure. The warning is that endings amplify emotion. Acknowledge the sadness, but do not let it distort judgment. If one returns to inner balance, both meeting and parting can be endured well.

overviewZh: 萃卦，上兑下坤，泽在地上，万物流聚，故名“萃”。此卦的核心不是单纯的人多势众，而是以中心为依归、以诚信为纽带、以秩序完成聚合。卦辞言“王假有庙”，指出真正的凝聚，必须有共同信念与庄重仪式，像君王入宗庙那样，使人心有所归。萃也意味着资源、人物、机会在一时集中，因此既有发展契机，也隐藏纷争与失控风险。聚而无主则乱，聚而有德则亨，所以又说“利见大人，利贞”。在现实中，萃卦常对应团队组建、社群整合、家族聚会、资本汇流、项目协作等局面。它提醒人们：当外部条件成熟、众人愿意靠拢时，最重要的不是扩张速度，而是建立公信、明确规则、安顿情绪，并提前防备意外。如此，聚集才能转化为持续力量，而非短暂喧闹。
overviewEn: Hexagram 45, Gathering Together, is formed by Lake above Earth. Water settling upon the earth suggests people, resources, and intentions coming into one place. The heart of this hexagram is not merely numerical strength, but meaningful convergence under a shared center. The Judgment points to the king approaching the ancestral temple, implying that true gathering requires reverence, legitimacy, and a common source of trust. When people assemble without direction, confusion follows; when they unite around virtue and clear purpose, prosperity becomes possible. Thus the hexagram emphasizes meeting the great person and remaining correct. In modern life, this pattern appears in team building, family reunions, public leadership, investment concentration, and collective projects. Gathering magnifies both opportunity and tension. It can produce support, momentum, and influence, yet it can also expose insecurity, rivalry, or hidden disorder. Therefore the hexagram teaches that leadership, sincerity, ritual, and preparedness are essential. One must organize the crowd, steady emotions, and anticipate risk before pursuing expansion. Properly handled, concentration becomes strength; mishandled, it becomes noise and fragmentation.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“萃：亨。王假有庙，利见大人，亨，利贞。用大牲吉，利有攸往。”《彖传》进一步解释：“萃，聚也；顺以说，刚中而应，故聚也。王假有庙，致孝享也；利见大人亨，聚以正也；用大牲吉，利有攸往，顺天命也。”可见，萃卦之所以亨通，不只因为人事汇集，更因为聚合具备三重条件：其一，“顺以说”，下坤为顺，上兑为悦，说明群体聚合须以柔顺承载、以和悦感召；其二，“刚中而应”，九五居中得正，象征有德有位的核心人物能够回应众心；其三，“聚以正”，聚会必须以正道为原则，不能靠私欲、煽动或权宜维系。宗庙之象尤其重要，它代表共同记忆、共同价值与庄严秩序。所谓“用大牲吉”，不是奢华表象，而是说明在重大凝聚时要有足够诚意与正式投入。故此卦告诫人们：能聚众者未必成事，唯有以德立中心、以礼安人心、以正定方向，聚集才真正转化为亨通。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says: “Gathering Together. Success. The king approaches his temple. It is beneficial to see the great person. Success. Perseverance is beneficial. To use great offerings brings good fortune. It is favorable to have somewhere to go.” The Tuan Commentary explains: “Gathering means assembling. Through compliance and joy, with firm centrality and response, therefore there is gathering. The king approaching the temple means extending filial devotion and offering. Beneficial to see the great person and success means gathering through correctness. Great offerings bring good fortune, and having somewhere to go accords with the mandate of Heaven.” This commentary shows that success in Gathering depends on more than people simply coming together. First, “compliance and joy” indicates that unity is sustained by receptivity below and goodwill above. Second, “firm centrality and response” points to a stable, morally grounded center—represented by the strong fifth line—that can answer collective needs. Third, gathering must be “through correctness,” not through manipulation, fear, or temporary passion. The temple image is crucial: it symbolizes shared memory, legitimacy, and sacred order. The mention of great offerings does not praise extravagance, but stresses sincerity and full commitment in moments of major consolidation. Hexagram 45 therefore teaches that leadership must be ethical, ceremonial, and purpose-driven. Only when people gather around trust, right principle, and a worthy center can collective concentration turn into lasting success.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“泽上于地，萃；君子以除戎器，戒不虞。”泽在地上，水气汇聚而润下，象征人群、物资、声势向一处集中。聚集本身带来活力，也带来压力：人多则意见杂，资源聚则容易引发争夺，局势热闹则更需防范突变。因此君子观此卦象，不只看到“合众”的表面繁盛，更会提前“除戎器，戒不虞”，即整顿武备、清理隐患、预防不测。这里的“戎器”既可指真实的防卫准备，也可引申为制度、预案、纪律与风险管理。萃之时最怕沉醉于热闹，而忘了群体中的不安与暗流。故此象告诉我们，真正高明的组织者，在众人欢聚之际，仍保持警觉、设定边界、安顿秩序，使聚集不致演变为混乱。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: “The lake rises over the earth: Gathering. Thus the noble one puts away weapons and guards against the unforeseen.” A lake above the earth suggests accumulation and concentration. Moisture gathers, influence gathers, and people gather. Yet concentration is never purely benign. When many people come together, interests multiply, emotions intensify, and small tensions can quickly become disorder. Therefore the noble person does not merely celebrate unity; he prepares for what may go wrong. “Putting away weapons” and “guarding against the unforeseen” implies organizing defenses, clarifying rules, and managing risk before trouble appears. In a broader sense, weapons here include structures of discipline, contingency plans, and practical safeguards. The image teaches that gatherings require stewardship. Moments of enthusiasm and common purpose are precisely when hidden frictions can be overlooked. Wise leadership sees both the vitality and the vulnerability of concentration. It preserves harmony not by naïve optimism, but by readiness, boundaries, and preventive care.
modernApplicationZh: 事业方面，萃卦适合团队整编、项目会战、资源整合、品牌联盟与组织动员。此时关键不是个人单打独斗，而是找到核心人物、统一目标、明确分工，让众力归于一处。感情方面，萃象征关系中的靠拢、团圆与共同经营，适合谈婚论嫁、修复联系、推动双方家庭互动；但若彼此只是情绪性聚合、缺乏长期共识，也容易热闹之后生出失望。财运方面，萃有资金汇聚、人脉带财、合作得利之象，适合联合投资、客户集中、渠道拓展，但必须防范因利益聚集引起的争执、分配不均与盲目跟风。健康方面，萃提示关注因饮食聚会、情绪压抑、作息失衡而带来的脾胃、湿气、循环系统问题，也提醒在群体生活或高频社交中注意感染与过劳。总体而言，萃卦在现代的启示是：一切“聚”的机会都值得珍惜，但越是人气旺、资源多、机会密集，越要靠制度、诚意与节制来维系。把人心聚拢之后，还要让它稳得住、走得远，才算真正得萃之道。
modernApplicationEn: In career matters, Hexagram 45 favors team formation, campaign-style execution, resource integration, organizational mobilization, and strategic partnerships. Success comes not from isolated talent but from identifying a reliable center, aligning objectives, and channeling many efforts into one coordinated direction. In relationships, it points to reunion, emotional convergence, and building life together. It is favorable for commitment, reconciliation, and family involvement, yet warns that emotional closeness without shared principles may fade after the excitement passes. Financially, Gathering suggests concentrated opportunity through networks, pooled capital, collaborative ventures, and customer accumulation. However, where money gathers, disputes may also gather, so transparent allocation and disciplined judgment are essential. In health, the hexagram advises attention to issues caused by social excess, irregular routines, emotional crowding, dampness, digestion, and circulatory strain. It also suggests caution regarding infection, burnout, or depletion in highly social environments. Overall, the modern lesson of Gathering is clear: concentrated momentum is powerful, but it must be supported by trust, structure, and restraint. Bringing people together is only the first step; sustaining cohesion and directing it wisely is the real work.
historicalStoryZh: 可借周公制礼作乐来理解萃卦精神。周初天下甫定，诸侯、宗族、旧民、新附之众并起，若无共同礼制，人心虽聚，秩序难成。周公并未只靠武力压服，而是通过宗庙祭祀、封建秩序、礼乐制度，把分散而复杂的力量纳入同一文明结构之中。这正与“王假有庙”“聚以正”的精神相合：先立共同尊崇，再定名分次序，众人方能聚而不乱。礼不是空文，而是让群体共享意义；祭不是形式，而是让政权获得正当性。萃卦所强调的，不是简单召集更多人，而是让聚合有中心、有名义、有节度。周公之所以能成就周室长治，正因为他懂得：真正强大的组织，不在于人数多少，而在于是否能把众心安放于同一个秩序之内。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical parallel for Hexagram 45 is the Duke of Zhou’s establishment of rites and music in the early Zhou dynasty. After conquest, many forces had been brought together—regional lords, clans, former subjects, and newly aligned peoples. Mere military control could not create lasting unity. The Duke of Zhou therefore shaped a shared order through ancestral worship, political hierarchy, and ritual institutions. This reflects the hexagram’s themes of “the king approaching the temple” and “gathering through correctness.” He did not simply assemble people; he gave their assembly legitimacy, sequence, and meaning. Ritual was not empty ceremony, but a way to align memory, identity, and obligation. Sacrifice was not display, but an expression of sincere participation in a larger moral order. The power of Gathering lies in this transformation: many separate elements become one community because they recognize a worthy center. The Duke of Zhou’s achievement shows that enduring unity depends less on force than on credible structure and shared reverence.
relatedHexagramsNote: 萃与第8卦“比”皆主聚合，但比偏重亲附与结盟，萃则强调在更大规模中建立中心与秩序。萃之后为第46卦“升”，说明聚集若能守正经营，便会由汇合转入上升发展；若聚而失序，则反易生乱。

judgmentZh: 升：元亨，用见大人，勿恤，南征吉。
judgmentEn: Pushing Upward has supreme success. One must see the great man. Fear not. Departure toward the south brings good fortune.
imageZh: 地中生木，升；君子以顺德，积小以高大。
imageEn: Within the earth, wood grows: the image of Pushing Upward. Thus the superior man of devoted character heaps up small things in order to achieve something high and great.
interpretationZh: 升卦象征上升，木在地中生长向上。顺应自然之德，积少成多，由小而大，循序渐进。
interpretationEn: Sheng symbolizes upward growth. Wood grows within the earth, rising steadily. Accumulate small gains to achieve greatness through gradual progress.

lines.textZh: 初六：允升，大吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Pushing upward that meets with confidence brings great good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 诚信上升，大吉大利。初始阶段得到信任，顺利上进。
lines.interpretationEn: Ascending with trust and confidence. Great good fortune at the outset.
lines.detailZh: “允升，大吉。”初六居升之始，以阴柔而得众信，“允”有诚信、允可之义。此爻说明一个人刚开始上升时，最重要的不是锋芒，而是可信、谦逊、得人心。起步阶段若能顺势而行，态度真诚，不自矜、不躁进，就会得到外界认可，因此大吉。
lines.detailEn: “Pushing upward with trust. Great good fortune.” The first line stands at the very beginning of ascent. Being soft and receptive, it gains confidence from others. The key word suggests trustworthiness and acceptance. At the start of advancement, what matters most is not brilliance or force, but sincerity, humility, and the ability to win support. When one begins in this spirit, progress is greatly auspicious.

lines.textZh: 九二：孚乃利用禴，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 心怀诚信，即使简单的祭祀也有利，无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: With sincerity, even a small offering furthers. No blame.
lines.detailZh: “孚乃利用禴，无咎。”九二以阳居中，内有诚信，中正不偏。禴是简薄而诚敬的祭礼，表示不在形式繁盛，而在内心真实。此爻告诫人在上升过程中，只要守住真诚与本分，即便条件简约，也足以通达神明与人心，所以无咎。
lines.detailEn: “With sincerity, it is beneficial to use the simple offering. No blame.” The second line is firm yet central, representing inner truth and balance. The sparse sacrificial rite symbolizes simplicity joined with genuine reverence. In advancement, elaborate display is less important than authentic intention. If one remains centered and sincere, even modest means are sufficient, and there is no blame.

lines.textZh: 九三：升虚邑。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: One pushes upward into an empty city.
lines.interpretationZh: 上升进入空虚之城，畅通无阻，毫无障碍。
lines.interpretationEn: Pushing upward into an empty city. No obstacles on the path.
lines.detailZh: “升虚邑。”九三阳刚得势，上升迅速，如进入空无阻碍的城邑。此爻象征时机顺畅、前路开阔，推进几乎没有明显阻拦。但也暗含提醒：局势虽顺，仍须守正，不可因容易而轻忽根基，否则“虚邑”也可能意味着外实内虚。
lines.detailEn: “He pushes upward into an empty city.” The third line suggests strong momentum and a path suddenly opening with little resistance, as if entering an undefended town. It symbolizes a favorable opportunity in which progress becomes easy. Yet it also carries a caution: when ascent feels effortless, one must still remain grounded and principled, lest apparent openness conceal inner emptiness.

lines.textZh: 六四：王用亨于岐山，吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The king offers him Mount Ch'i. Good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 君王在岐山祭祀，吉利无咎。顺应天命，恭敬奉献。
lines.interpretationEn: The king makes offerings at Mount Ch'i. Good fortune and no blame through devoted service.
lines.detailZh: “王用亨于岐山，吉，无咎。”六四近君位而承上启下，以柔顺之德事上而成礼。岐山象征周室兴起之基，此爻强调上升不可忘本，当以敬天法祖、修德行礼来巩固事业。能以正礼承接更高位置，则吉而无咎。
lines.detailEn: “The king makes an offering on Mount Qi. Good fortune. No blame.” The fourth line stands near power and represents advancement through reverence, ritual propriety, and remembrance of foundations. Mount Qi evokes the Zhou ancestral base, suggesting that real elevation must remain rooted in origin, gratitude, and cultivated virtue. Rise sustained by proper conduct brings good fortune without blame.

lines.textZh: 六五：贞吉，升阶。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. One pushes upward by steps.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，一步一步稳步上升。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune. Ascend step by step in steady progress.
lines.detailZh: “贞吉，升阶。”六五居尊位而能守正，是升卦中最理想的治理状态。“升阶”不是暴进，而是一阶一阶安稳登临。此爻说明身处高位时，仍须以贞正为本，循序推进制度、责任与德望，方能吉祥。
lines.detailEn: “Perseverance brings good fortune. One rises step by step.” The fifth line occupies the honored place and shows the ideal form of ascent: dignified, correct, and measured. Rising by steps means orderly advancement rather than reckless climbing. Even at a high level, success depends on continued integrity and disciplined progression.

lines.textZh: 上六：冥升，利于不息之贞。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Pushing upward in darkness. It furthers one to be unremittingly persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 在昏暗中上升，利于不懈地坚守正道。盲目上进需以恒心自持。
lines.interpretationEn: Ascending in darkness. Unceasing perseverance is needed. Blind ambition requires steadfast discipline.
lines.detailZh: “冥升，利于不息之贞。”上六升至极处，有昏冥中仍欲再升之象。“冥升”可解为在不明形势下勉力上进，既有可贵的毅力，也有过犹不及的危险。故爻辞不言大吉，只言“利于不息之贞”，意在提醒：到了高处，更需要持续自省，以正固守，而非盲目攀求。
lines.detailEn: “Pushing upward in darkness. It is beneficial to maintain unceasing perseverance.” The top line shows ascent carried to its extreme. One may still wish to rise further even when clarity is fading. This contains both admirable persistence and the danger of excess. Therefore the line does not promise great fortune outright; instead, it advises constant, upright perseverance. At great heights, one must value clarity and self-restraint over blind ambition.

overviewZh: 升卦，上坤下巽，象为地中生木。木本柔顺，却能凭借向上的生机穿土而出，因此“升”不是骤然拔擢，而是循序渐进、由微至著的成长过程。此卦强调以柔顺之德配合坚定之志，在时机合宜时依附贤者、顺应正道，便可由低处稳步上行。它所揭示的并非投机式跃迁，而是长期积累后的自然提升：德行愈厚，根基愈稳；行动愈谦，前途愈广。对处于起步、转型、晋升阶段的人而言，升卦提醒人们不必忧惧暂时的卑微与缓慢，只要持续精进、善于借力、守正不躁，终能越阶而上，抵达更高的位置与格局。
overviewEn: Hexagram 46, Pushing Upward, is formed by Earth above and Wind/Wood below. Its image is wood growing from within the earth: soft in substance, yet steadily capable of rising through patience and inner vitality. This hexagram does not describe a sudden leap, but gradual advancement built on accumulated effort, humility, and alignment with the proper time. Progress here comes through sincerity, steady cultivation, and the willingness to seek guidance from the wise rather than relying on force alone. It teaches that true elevation is organic: what grows well rises well. For those at the beginning of a journey, in transition, or seeking promotion, this hexagram offers reassurance. One need not fear a low starting point or slow pace. When character is cultivated, small steps are honored, and one proceeds with calm perseverance, advancement becomes not only possible but natural and auspicious.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“升：元亨，用见大人，勿恤，南征吉。”其义在于升进之道本可大亨，但关键不在逞强冒进，而在“用见大人”——得贤者提携、与正道同行。彖传说：“柔以时升，巽而顺，刚中而应，是以大亨。用见大人，勿恤，有庆也。南征吉，志行也。”所谓“柔以时升”，说明升卦的力量来自合时而进，虽柔而不弱；“巽而顺”则指出其方法是谦逊、顺势、不逆天时人情。九二刚中而与六五相应，象征内有诚信与中正，外有接纳与扶持，故可成其升。所谓“南征吉”，并非单指方向，而是象征向光明、文明、正大之地前行。故此卦告诫人们：上进之际不必忧虑，只要所求合义、所依得人、所行守正，提升不仅无害，反而是天时人事共同成就的吉庆。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says, “Pushing Upward. Supreme success. One should see the great person. Do not worry. Going south brings good fortune.” Its meaning is that upward progress can indeed lead to great success, but not through aggression or impatience. The core lies in “seeing the great person” — seeking guidance, support, or alignment with those of wisdom and moral authority. The Tuan Commentary explains: “The soft rises at the proper time. Gentle and compliant, with the firm in the center responding, therefore there is great success. To see the great person and not worry means there is blessing. Going south is auspicious because the will is carried out.” “The soft rises at the proper time” shows that advancement here comes through timing and receptivity, not force. “Gentle and compliant” points to a method of humility and harmony with circumstance. The central firm line responding upward suggests inner sincerity meeting outer recognition. “Going south” symbolically means moving toward clarity, culture, and brightness. Thus the hexagram teaches that one need not fear advancement when intention is upright, timing is ripe, and one proceeds with guidance and moral purpose.
imageDetailZh: 象曰：“地中生木，升；君子以顺德，积小以高大。”木在地中，并非一蹴而就地冲出地表，而是在幽微处扎根、生发、渐次上升。这正是升卦最核心的形象：真正的成长多始于看不见之处，先有内在根柢，再有外在高度。君子观此卦象，明白成就并不来自空想与躁进，而来自顺应本性、培育德行、日积月累。所谓“积小以高大”，不是轻视微小，而是知道每一寸增长都值得珍惜。今日多一分学习，明日多一分器识；今日多一分忍耐，来日便多一分承担大任的能力。升卦的象意提醒人们：若想真正向上，就要像树木一样，把功夫用在根上，把耐心放在过程里。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Wood grows up from within the earth: Pushing Upward. Thus the noble person, by complying with virtue, accumulates small things in order to attain what is high and great.” Wood beneath the earth does not burst upward instantly. It first takes root in darkness, gathers strength quietly, and only then rises. This is the essential image of the hexagram: real growth often begins where it cannot yet be seen. Height depends on depth. From this, the noble person learns that achievement is not born from restless ambition alone, but from aligning with virtue, accepting natural process, and building patiently from the small to the great. “Accumulating small things” means honoring incremental progress rather than despising humble beginnings. A little more learning, discipline, sincerity, or endurance each day eventually becomes visible stature. The image reminds us that if we wish to rise truly and sustainably, we must work like a tree — strengthening roots, respecting time, and allowing elevation to emerge from inner development.
modernApplicationZh: 事业上，升卦最利于循序晋升、能力沉淀与贵人扶持，适合从基层做起、逐步承担更大责任；若急于求成、越级冒进，反而易失其位。感情上，此卦象征关系可逐渐升温，宜以真诚、耐心、尊重来培育信任，尤其适合从平淡相处走向稳定承诺，不宜用情绪施压。财运上，升卦主稳步累积，利于长期投资、正业增收、口碑经营，不利一夜暴富式投机；小利能聚，大财方成。健康上，则提示身体调养重在渐进，尤其要关注脾胃、肝气、压力导致的气机不畅，规律作息与长期保养比短期猛补更有效。总体而言，升卦在现代生活中的启示是：凡事向上，靠的是长期主义、内在修为和正确支持系统。慢不是坏，稳才是真正可持续的快。
modernApplicationEn: In career matters, Hexagram 46 favors gradual promotion, skill accumulation, and support from mentors or influential allies. It is especially auspicious for those willing to begin at the foundation and grow into larger responsibilities. Trying to skip stages or force rapid advancement may undermine the very rise one seeks. In relationships, this hexagram suggests feelings can deepen steadily through sincerity, patience, and mutual respect. It is favorable for bonds that mature from modest beginnings into stable commitment, but not for emotional pressure or rushed expectations. Financially, it points to steady accumulation: long-term investing, reliable work, and reputation-based growth are favored, while speculative schemes promising instant wealth are not. Small gains, properly gathered, become meaningful prosperity. In health, the message is similar: improvement comes through consistent habits rather than extreme measures. Attention to digestion, stress regulation, and overall balance is important. In modern life, Pushing Upward teaches that true progress depends on long-term discipline, inner cultivation, and the right support structure. Slow is not failure; steady is often the deepest form of success.
historicalStoryZh: 升卦常被联想到周人由岐山兴起的历史气象。爻辞“六四：王用亨于岐山，吉，无咎”直接点出岐山，令人想到周文王、周武王积德行仁、礼贤下士，由一方诸侯逐步壮大，最终奠定王业。周之兴并非暴起，而是长期经营民心、修明政治、顺天应人的结果，正如木生地中，先厚其根，再见其高。其所以能“升”，不在武力一端，而在德业日进、众望所归。这个历史意象极好地说明升卦精神：真正可久可大的上升，往往来自深厚积累与顺德而行，而不是一时侥幸。
historicalStoryEn: Hexagram 46 is often associated with the rise of the Zhou from Mount Qi. The fourth line says, “The king makes an offering on Mount Qi. Good fortune. No blame,” directly evoking the cultural memory of the Zhou kings, especially King Wen and King Wu, who gradually built moral legitimacy, attracted worthy people, and expanded their influence step by step. Their ascent was not a reckless seizure of power, but the result of long cultivation of virtue, good governance, and alignment with Heaven and the people. Like wood growing within the earth, their strength first matured inwardly before it became visibly great. This historical image perfectly expresses the spirit of Pushing Upward: lasting elevation is rarely sudden. It comes through accumulated merit, proper conduct, and steady growth that eventually wins broad support.
relatedHexagramsNote: 升卦与第11卦“泰”都主通达上进，但泰偏重天地交而万物通，升则重个人或群体由下而上的渐进成长。与第35卦“晋”相比，晋有日出地上的显明进展，升则更强调根基、积累与借贤而升，属于内蓄而后发的上行之道。
references: 《彖传》：柔以时升，巽而顺，刚中而应，是以大亨。用见大人，勿恤，有庆也。南征吉，志行也。
《象传》：地中生木，升；君子以顺德，积小以高大。

judgmentZh: 困：亨，贞，大人吉，无咎，有言不信。
judgmentEn: Oppression. Success. Perseverance. The great man brings about good fortune. No blame. When one has something to say, it is not believed.
imageZh: 泽无水，困；君子以致命遂志。
imageEn: There is no water in the lake: the image of Exhaustion. Thus the superior man stakes his life on following his will.
interpretationZh: 困卦象征困穷，泽中无水，枯竭之象。君子身处困境，宁可舍命也要实现志向，言语不被信任时以行动证明。
interpretationEn: Kun symbolizes oppression and exhaustion. The lake is without water. The superior man stakes his life on his convictions, proving himself through action when words are not believed.

lines.textZh: 初六：臀困于株木，入于幽谷，三岁不觌。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: One sits oppressed under a bare tree and strays into a gloomy valley. For three years one sees nothing.
lines.interpretationZh: 困坐于枯木之下，迷入幽暗山谷，三年不见光明。处境极为困顿。
lines.interpretationEn: Sitting oppressed under a bare tree, lost in a dark valley. For three years, no light. A time of deep hardship.
lines.detailZh: “臀困于株木，入于幽谷，三岁不觌。”初六阴柔居下，处困之始，既无援力，又失行动空间，如人臀部受制于枯木，进一步又陷入幽暗深谷，久久不得见人。此爻言困境初临时的闭塞与失联，象征判断失准、环境压抑、孤立无援。其警示在于：起初若方向错误，又缺乏外援，越挣扎越陷深处。宜先止步自保，避免盲动。
lines.detailEn: “One’s seat is oppressed by dead wood; one enters a dark valley and for three years sees no one.” The first line is weak and low, marking the beginning of oppression without support or room to move. The imagery suggests immobilization, isolation, and descent into a situation so obscure that help seems absent for a long time. It warns that at the onset of difficulty, wrong movement and poor judgment can deepen entrapment. The proper response is not frantic action but restraint, self-protection, and careful reassessment.

lines.textZh: 九二：困于酒食，朱绂方来，利用享祀，征凶，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One is oppressed while at meat and drink. The man with the scarlet knee bands is just coming. It furthers one to offer sacrifice. To set forth brings misfortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在酒食中受困，贵人即将到来。宜祭祀祈福，出征则凶，但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed amid plenty. A dignitary approaches. Offer sacrifice; do not march forth. No blame.
lines.detailZh: “困于酒食，朱绂方来，利用享祀，征凶，无咎。”九二刚中，有内在能力，却被安逸牵制，或被现实供养系统束缚，看似不缺，实则难展其志。“朱绂方来”表示外部名位、机会或援助将至，但尚未完全到位。此时适合整肃内心、敬慎行事，如祭祀般端正其诚；若贸然进取，则有凶险。守中不躁，故终无咎。
lines.detailEn: “Oppressed amid wine and food; vermilion robes are on the way. It is beneficial to make offerings. To advance brings misfortune, but no blame.” The second line is strong and centered, so it has real capacity, yet it is constrained by comfort, obligation, or dependence on an existing system. Outwardly there is provision, but inwardly there is frustration. Signs of recognition or assistance are approaching, though not yet fully present. This line advises reverence, preparation, and disciplined sincerity rather than ambitious advance. Premature action invites trouble; patient composure avoids blame.

lines.textZh: 六三：困于石，据于蒺藜，入于其宫，不见其妻，凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: One lets himself be oppressed by stone, and leans on thorns and thistles. He enters his house and does not see his wife. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 被巨石所困，倚靠荆棘，回到家中却不见妻子，凶险。进退失据，内外皆困。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed by stone, leaning on thorns. Entering home but not finding one's wife. Misfortune from being trapped inside and out.
lines.detailZh: “困于石，据于蒺藜，入于其宫，不见其妻，凶。”六三失中失正，处上下夹困之地，如坐于硬石，依于蒺藜，进退皆伤；回到本应安顿的居所，连最亲近的人也不相见。此爻描写的是因选择失当、性情偏激或处境恶化而导致的全面失和：外部刺痛，内部离散。其凶在于既不得势，又失根本依托。唯有深刻反省，停止自伤式决策，方可转机。
lines.detailEn: “Oppressed on stone, leaning on thorns; entering one’s house, one does not see one’s wife. Misfortune.” The third line is neither centered nor correct, caught in a highly unstable place. The images of stone and thorn-bushes evoke pain in both stillness and movement, while returning home without meeting one’s spouse signals estrangement even in the nearest sphere. This line depicts a crisis intensified by poor positioning, reactive choices, or inner disorder. External hardship has now penetrated personal foundations. Misfortune comes from losing both practical footing and relational support. Reflection and a halt to self-damaging behavior are essential.

lines.textZh: 九四：来徐徐，困于金车，吝，有终。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He comes very slowly, oppressed in a golden carriage. Humiliation, but the end is reached.
lines.interpretationZh: 行动迟缓，困于华贵的车中。虽有困窘，但终能达成目标。
lines.interpretationEn: Coming slowly, oppressed in a golden carriage. Humiliation, but the goal is eventually reached.
lines.detailZh: “来徐徐，困于金车，吝，有终。”九四以阳居阴，接近上位，想来援救却受身份、责任或制度所累，行动迟缓，如为华贵金车所困。这里的“吝”是过程中的局促与难堪，并非终局失败。说明人在接近转机时，常仍被条件牵制，不能立刻解困。只要不因迟滞而失志，虽进展缓慢，最终仍有结果。
lines.detailEn: “He comes very slowly, oppressed in a golden carriage. Humiliation, yet there is an end.” The fourth line stands near authority and potential relief, but movement is delayed by status, obligations, or the burdens of position itself. The golden carriage symbolizes privilege that also restricts. Embarrassment or frustration is present because help cannot arrive swiftly. Still, this is not final failure. The line teaches that even when release is near, conditions may slow the process. If one remains steady despite delay, the matter can still reach completion.

lines.textZh: 九五：劓刖，困于赤绂，乃徐有说，利用祭祀。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: His nose and feet are cut off. Oppression at the hands of the man with the purple knee bands. Joy comes softly. It furthers one to make offerings and libations.
lines.interpretationZh: 遭受刑罚之困，被权贵所压迫。慢慢地喜悦到来，宜以祭祀求解。
lines.interpretationEn: Nose and feet cut off, oppressed by authority. Joy comes gradually. Offerings and devotion bring relief.
lines.detailZh: “劓刖，困于赤绂，乃徐有说，利用祭祀。”九五居尊位而受困，显示高位者也可能遭受重压与掣肘，甚至像受刑般威望受损、手脚难展。“赤绂”象征权位束缚，责任越大，困感越深。但此爻得中，故能在迟缓中逐步解脱。“利用祭祀”表示回归庄敬、正本清源，以诚敬整顿秩序。此爻说明领导者的解困，不靠强行突破，而靠恢复正当性与中心稳定。
lines.detailEn: “Nose and feet cut off; oppressed by red insignia. Then slowly there is release. It is beneficial to make offerings.” The fifth line occupies the seat of rulership, showing that even those in high position can be severely constrained. The imagery of mutilation points to humiliation, limitation, and loss of free agency; the red insignia symbolize office, authority, and the burdens attached to them. Yet because this line is central, release becomes possible over time. Ritual offering here suggests returning to legitimacy, sincerity, and proper order. A leader escapes oppression not by forceful display, but by restoring moral center and rightful alignment.

lines.textZh: 上六：困于葛藟，于臲卼，曰动悔有悔，征吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: He is oppressed by creeping vines. He moves uncertainly and says, 'Movement brings remorse.' If one feels remorse over this and makes a start, good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 被葛藤缠绕所困，摇摆不定。若能反省悔悟并付诸行动，则前往吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed by entangling vines, wavering. If one repents and takes action, setting forth brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “困于葛藟，于臲卼，曰动悔。有悔，征吉。”上六处困之极，像被藤蔓缠绕，立于危惧不安之地。若仍畏缩不前，只会不断自言“行动必悔”，久困不出；但真正意识到自己的迟疑与悔意之后，反而可以转而前行，所以说“有悔，征吉”。此爻揭示困卦终点的关键：解困常始于承认自己的恐惧与错误，随后带着清醒去行动。
lines.detailEn: “Oppressed by creeping vines, on a precarious height. One says: movement brings regret. Yet there is regret, and setting forth brings good fortune.” The top line marks the extreme of oppression: entanglement, instability, and fear of any further step. One hesitates, convinced that action will only worsen matters. Yet the line turns precisely through conscious regret. Once one recognizes the paralysis, admits past error, and stops clinging to fear, movement becomes auspicious. The final lesson is that the end of oppression often begins not with certainty, but with honest recognition and courageous action after self-awareness.

overviewZh: 第47卦为困卦，上兑下坎，泽在上而水在下，本应润泽万物，却呈现泽中无水、资源枯竭之象，所以名为“困”。困不是单纯的失败，而是人在外受压、内有险阻、进退失据时的生命状态。卦辞却先言“亨”，说明真正的通达并不总来自顺境，反而常在艰难压迫中显出人格的重量。此卦强调守正、忍耐、节制与自我砥砺：言语未必被信任，才智未必立刻被承认，但大人能在穷厄之际不失其志，因此终得无咎。困卦所揭示的，不只是现实中的匮乏，更是精神上的考验：当外在援助稀少时，人是否还能守住原则、完成志向。故困卦是一卦讲逆境修身、以苦炼志、由窘迫走向内在成熟的经典卦象。
overviewEn: Hexagram 47, Kun, is formed by Dui above and Kan below: a marsh above water, yet the image is one of a lake drained dry. It signifies oppression, exhaustion, constraint, and the feeling of having no easy way forward. Yet the Judgment begins with success, showing that true progress does not always arise from ease; it may emerge through hardship that tests character. Kun describes a condition in which a person is pressed from outside and obstructed within, unable to rely on quick recognition, smooth communication, or immediate relief. Words may not be believed, efforts may seem delayed, and support may arrive slowly. Even so, the hexagram teaches that perseverance in what is right, inner sincerity, and disciplined endurance transform adversity into moral strength. It is not merely a picture of suffering, but of how one responds to it. The noble person in times of oppression does not collapse into resentment; instead, they refine resolve, preserve integrity, and fulfill purpose under pressure. Thus Kun is a profound meditation on constraint as a path to maturity.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“困：亨，贞，大人吉，无咎，有言不信。”困而曰亨，看似矛盾，实则指出困境中的亨通并非外在环境立刻改善，而是内在德性不被困住。彖传说：“困，刚揜也。险以说，困而不失其所亨，其唯君子乎！贞大人吉，以刚中也。有言不信，尚口乃穷也。”所谓“刚揜”，是阳刚之德受遮蔽，才志难伸；但卦内有坎险，外有兑悦，意味着君子虽处险境，仍能以平和自持，不改其正。大人之所以吉，在于居中守刚，不因受压而改节。至于“有言不信”，则提醒人在穷困时，多言无益，越想辩白越显窘迫，唯有以行动、品格与时间证明自己。困卦的判断不是教人消极忍受，而是要求在受困之际不失中心、不弃原则，以正固身，以德通命。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says: “Oppression. Success. Perseverance. The great person brings good fortune. No blame. Words are not believed.” Its paradox is essential: how can oppression contain success? The Tuanzhuan explains: “Oppression means the strong are covered. Amid danger there is joy; though oppressed, one does not lose the ground of success—this belongs only to the noble person. Perseverance of the great person brings good fortune because of firm centrality. Words are not believed because exalting speech leads to exhaustion.” Here, oppression is not denied; it is named as a condition in which strength, talent, or principle cannot easily manifest. Yet inner alignment remains possible. The combination of danger within and openness without suggests that one may endure hardship without losing composure. The “great person” is fortunate not because circumstances are easy, but because they remain centered, steady, and morally firm under pressure. The warning that words are not believed is equally important: in constrained times, explanations alone persuade no one. Complaints, self-defense, and rhetoric only deepen frustration. What carries weight is conduct, restraint, and lived integrity over time. Thus the Judgment teaches that in adversity, true success lies first in preserving the core of one’s character.
imageDetailZh: 象辞曰：“泽无水，困；君子以致命遂志。”泽本赖水而成，今泽中无水，外形尚在而内里已竭，正是困之象。此象说明人的困境往往不是骤然崩塌，而是长期消耗、内外失衡所致：表面仍维持体面，实则资源、情感、信心已近枯竭。君子观此，不是怨天尤人，而是“致命遂志”，即在穷厄中安顿生命，把命与志统一起来。必要时宁可承受代价，也不轻易放弃根本方向。困卦之象因此具有强烈的精神性：困不只是被环境围困，更是考验一个人是否能在看不见出路时仍保持志节。真正的解困，不先靠外在突破，而始于内在不散、不乱、不失其志。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: “There is no water in the marsh: Oppression. The noble person stakes life itself to fulfill the will.” A marsh exists through water; when the water is gone, the form remains but the sustaining substance is depleted. This captures oppression as a state of exhaustion—one may still appear functional outwardly while inward reserves have run dangerously low. The image points not only to external shortage, but to inner depletion, emotional fatigue, and prolonged pressure. The noble person, seeing this, does not surrender to bitterness. Instead, they “devote life to fulfilling purpose,” meaning they bind life to principle and remain faithful to what matters most, even when conditions are severe. This does not glorify suffering for its own sake; rather, it insists that adversity reveals whether one’s purpose is superficial or real. In Kun, the first work of release is inward: to keep from scattering, panicking, or abandoning one’s true direction when visible support disappears.
modernApplicationZh: 事业：困卦在事业上多指资源紧缩、推进受阻、上级不信或团队疲惫。此时不宜逞强冒进，也不宜只靠解释争辩，应先稳住基本盘、修补流程、保存实力，等待可信成果逐步建立信任。感情：感情中的“困”常表现为沟通受阻、误会加深、彼此都有委屈却说不清。越急于证明自己，越可能让关系更僵，因此更适合用耐心、陪伴与实际改变化解隔阂。财运：财务上宜守不宜攻，可能出现现金流紧张、回款变慢、投入难见效等情况，应减少高风险投资，控制负债，优先处理刚性支出。健康：困卦也提示长期疲劳、睡眠不足、精神压抑、内分泌或泌尿系统失衡等问题，关键不在硬撑，而在节奏调整、情绪疏导与持续修复。整体而言，此卦现代应用的核心，是在压力期降低无效消耗，用纪律替代焦躁，用韧性穿越低谷。
modernApplicationEn: Career: In professional life, Kun often points to tight resources, stalled plans, weak support, or a sense that one’s efforts are not fully trusted. This is not the moment for reckless expansion or endless self-justification. Better results come from stabilizing essentials, improving processes, conserving strength, and letting credibility be rebuilt through visible outcomes. Relationships: In love or family life, oppression may appear as emotional distance, repeated misunderstanding, or the feeling that both sides are hurt yet unable to communicate clearly. The more one tries to force resolution through argument, the worse things may become. Patience, consistency, and concrete change work better than dramatic declarations. Finances: Financially, this hexagram favors defense over aggression. Cash flow may tighten, returns may slow, and speculative moves can worsen pressure. Reduce unnecessary exposure, manage debt carefully, and protect necessities first. Health: Kun also warns of burnout, poor sleep, stress accumulation, low mood, and conditions linked to chronic depletion. Recovery depends less on pushing harder and more on rhythm, rest, emotional regulation, and sustained repair. Overall, the modern lesson of Kun is to reduce waste under pressure, replace agitation with discipline, and pass through the low point with integrity intact.
historicalStoryZh: 困卦常使人联想到文王被拘羑里之事。周文王身处囚困之中，外受强权压制，内怀天下之志，正是“困而不失其所亨”的典型写照。相传他在艰危中推演《周易》，并非坐待命运转机，而是在受限环境里完成精神与思想的凝聚。其处境正对应困卦所言“有言不信”：在强权之下，多辩无益，惟有蓄德守正，以时间证明天命所归。后来文王终能转危为机，奠定周室兴起之基。这个故事说明，困并不只是压迫，更可能是伟大事业前的锻炼期；真正决定结局的，不是眼前受困多深，而是受困时是否仍能守志、蓄势、待时而发。
historicalStoryEn: Hexagram 47 is often illuminated through the story of King Wen of Zhou during his imprisonment at Youli. He was politically constrained, watched by hostile power, and unable to act openly, yet he did not lose his larger purpose. This embodies the line from the Commentary that one may be oppressed and still not lose the basis of success. Tradition holds that in confinement he deepened reflection on the Changes, turning external restriction into inner clarification rather than despair. His condition also reflects the warning that “words are not believed”: under domination, argument alone had little force. What mattered was steadfastness, moral discipline, and preserving vision until the proper time arrived. In the end, his hardship became part of the foundation for the rise of Zhou. The story shows that oppression is not only a period of suffering; it can be the forge in which leadership, patience, and destiny are tested and refined.
relatedHexagramsNote: 困卦与第48卦井卦前后相承，困极则思本源，故井象征在枯竭后重新汲取生命之水；与第29卦坎卦也相通，皆言险难，但坎重在险境本身，困更强调受压后的匮乏与坚持。读困卦宜连看井卦，体会“守志”之后如何“复源”。
references: 《彖传》：困，刚揜也。险以说，困而不失其所亨，其唯君子乎！贞大人吉，以刚中也。有言不信，尚口乃穷也。
《象传》：泽无水，困；君子以致命遂志。

judgmentZh: 井：改邑不改井，无丧无得，往来井井。汔至，亦未繘井，羸其瓶，凶。
judgmentEn: The Well. The town may be changed, but the well cannot be changed. It neither decreases nor increases. They come and go and draw from the well. If one gets down almost to the water and the rope does not go all the way, or the jug breaks, it brings misfortune.
imageZh: 木上有水，井；君子以劳民劝相。
imageEn: Water over wood: the image of the Well. Thus the superior man encourages the people at their work, and exhorts them to help one another.
interpretationZh: 井卦象征水井，木上有水，汲水而上。井水不变，养育万民。君子效法井德，勤劳利民，互助互勉。
interpretationEn: Jing symbolizes the well. Water over wood draws nourishment upward. The well is constant, sustaining all. The superior man encourages mutual aid and diligent service.

lines.textZh: 初六：井泥不食，旧井无禽。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: One does not drink the mud of the well. No animals come to an old well.
lines.interpretationZh: 井底淤泥不可饮用，废弃的旧井连禽兽都不来。才德荒废则无人亲近。
lines.interpretationEn: The mud of the well is not drunk. No animals come to an old well. Neglected talent attracts no one.
lines.detailZh: “井泥不食，旧井无禽。”初六居井之始，阴柔失位，如井底淤泥积塞，井水虽存却不可饮用，连飞禽也不来停栖。象征一个人或一个系统处于荒废、闭塞、失去功能的状态。其问题不在资源绝对匮乏，而在长期失修、价值被埋没。此爻提醒应先清理沉积的问题、旧习与惰性，否则再好的根基也形同废井。
lines.detailEn: “Mud in the well: no one drinks from it. An old well attracts no birds.” At the beginning, the well is blocked at its very source. The image is one of neglect, stagnation, and buried usefulness. The resource exists, yet it cannot serve life because it has not been maintained. This line often points to wasted talent, outdated systems, or emotional stagnation that make a person or structure inaccessible. The first task is cleansing and reopening what has been allowed to decay.

lines.textZh: 九二：井谷射鲋，瓮敝漏。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: At the wellhole one shoots fishes. The jug is broken and leaks.
lines.interpretationZh: 井中只能射小鱼，水瓮破漏。才能未得善用，有才而无用武之地。
lines.interpretationEn: Shooting fish at the wellhole. The jug is broken and leaks. Talent is wasted without proper application.
lines.detailZh: “井谷射鲋，瓮敝漏。”九二有阳刚之才，却未得其正用，像有人不去正经汲井，反在井谷射取小鱼；又如盛水之瓮破漏，所得难存。此爻言才力用偏、器具不良，虽有行动却难成其功。现实中常表现为抓小失大、资源配置错误，或方法、容器、制度出了漏洞，导致努力无法沉淀成果。
lines.detailEn: “At the well hollow one shoots small fish. The water jar is broken and leaks.” Here there is energy and initiative, but it is misdirected. Instead of drawing water for nourishment, one chases trifles; instead of preserving what is gained, the container loses it. This line warns against misapplied talent, poor prioritization, and faulty tools or systems. Effort is present, but because the means are flawed, value cannot be retained.

lines.textZh: 九三：井渫不食，为我心恻，可用汲，王明，并受其福。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The well is cleaned, but no one drinks from it. This is my heart's sorrow, for one might draw from it. If the king were clear-minded, good fortune might be enjoyed in common.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水已清却无人饮用，令人心痛。若遇明君赏识，则众人共享福泽。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is clean but unused. A sorrowful waste. If the king were wise, all could share its blessings.
lines.detailZh: “井渫不食，为我心恻，可用汲，王明，并受其福。”九三刚而得位，井已淘净，却一时未被使用，令人惋惜。此爻是怀才未遇、良策未行之象，但同时也说明价值已经具备，只待明主或识者启用。一旦被正确提拔与运用，不仅个人受益，更能惠及众人。它鼓励人在未被看见时继续自修，使自己真正达到“可用汲”的状态。
lines.detailEn: “The well is cleaned, yet no one drinks from it. This makes my heart ache. It can be drawn from. When the king is enlightened, all receive its blessing.” This is a powerful line of unrealized worth. The source has been purified and is ready, but recognition has not yet arrived. It often describes capable people, good institutions, or sound ideas awaiting proper use. The sorrow here is not failure but delay. Once wise leadership or clear judgment appears, the benefit extends far beyond the individual.

lines.textZh: 六四：井甃，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The well is being lined. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 用砖石修砌井壁，无过失。修缮自身，打好基础。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is being lined with stone. No blame. Repair and strengthen the foundation.
lines.detailZh: “井甃，无咎。”六四言以砖石修砌井壁，使井体稳固，不致坍塌。此爻重在“修结构”而非急取水，象征当资源之源已有基础时，还需完善制度、边界、流程与安全性。看似保守，实则必要。无咎之义在于守分而补偏，不逞一时之功，却为长期使用创造条件。
lines.detailEn: “The well is lined. No blame.” This line concerns structural repair. Before a well can serve reliably, its walls must be reinforced so that the source remains protected and usable. In life, this means building sound frameworks—rules, boundaries, infrastructure, and maintenance practices. It may not look impressive, but it prevents collapse. There is no blame because proper support is itself a form of wisdom.

lines.textZh: 九五：井冽，寒泉食。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: In the well there is a clear, cold spring from which one can drink.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水清冽，寒泉可饮。才德兼备，泽被众人。
lines.interpretationEn: A clear, cold spring in the well from which all may drink. Virtue and talent benefit everyone.
lines.detailZh: “井冽，寒泉食。”九五居尊得中，如井水清冽甘美，可供众人饮用，是井卦中最成熟的施用之象。这里代表德才充实而能公正施惠，资源洁净且流通，领导者或核心人物具备稳定输出价值的能力。此爻最宜理解为“本源既清，功用自显”，也是组织与人格都达到可信赖状态的标志。
lines.detailEn: “The well is clear. One drinks from its cold spring.” This is the noble fulfillment of the hexagram. The source is pure, accessible, and nourishing. In human terms, it indicates mature virtue, dependable leadership, or a system whose value is both real and available. There is no waste, blockage, or distortion here. The line shows what happens when inner quality and outer usefulness are fully aligned.

lines.textZh: 上六：井收勿幕，有孚元吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: One draws from the well without hindrance. It is dependable. Supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水汲取无遮盖，人人可用。诚信可靠，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is open and dependable for all. With sincerity and reliability comes supreme good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “井收勿幕，有孚元吉。”上六言井功既成，当开放共享，不可覆盖遮蔽。若以诚信待人，让众人自由汲取，则大吉。此爻是井卦的最高境界：资源不仅被修好，而且不私藏、不垄断，真正成为公共福祉。对个人而言，是成熟后愿意分享经验与德行；对组织而言，则是建立可信、开放、可持续的供给机制。
lines.detailEn: “The well is drawn from. Do not cover it. With sincerity, great good fortune.” At the top, the well is fully realized as a shared blessing. Once the source is sound, it should not be hidden, hoarded, or blocked. Trust and openness allow the well to fulfill its highest purpose. For individuals, this suggests generous sharing of wisdom and capacity; for institutions, it points to transparent and reliable access to what sustains the community.

overviewZh: 井卦象征文明共同体中稳定而长久的滋养之源。卦体下巽上坎，木入于水，如以桔槔汲井，又见井泉因人用而成其德。井与城邑不同，村落可迁，制度可变，但真正能持续供养众人的根本资源、公共伦理与人格本源，不应轻易废弃。此卦所讲的不只是水井，更是一个人内在的德性储备、组织中的制度基础、社会里的公共服务体系。它提醒人们：真正重要的东西往往不喧哗，却承担着长期的供养职责。若井淤塞、井器破败，即使近在眼前也无法受益；若能淘井修甃、诚信开放，则人人皆可汲取而蒙福。井卦因此强调“修本”“利众”“成用”，主张回到根基、整治资源、使潜藏价值真正服务于人。
overviewEn: Hexagram 48, The Well, speaks of a stable and enduring source of nourishment within human life and society. Its structure, Wind/Wood below and Water above, suggests wood entering water, like the mechanism used to draw from a well. Villages may move and external conditions may change, yet the well remains a constant necessity. In this sense, the hexagram is not only about a literal well but also about inner virtue, institutional foundations, shared knowledge, and public resources that sustain a community over time. The teaching is that what truly supports life is often quiet, old, and easily overlooked. If the well is clogged, neglected, or if the vessel is broken, the resource exists but cannot benefit anyone. If it is cleaned, lined, and opened in trust, it becomes a blessing to all. The Well therefore concerns returning to fundamentals, maintaining useful structures, and making hidden value available in practical form. It asks whether one’s gifts, systems, and moral reserves are merely present, or truly accessible and life-giving.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞“改邑不改井，无丧无得，往来井井”，指出井作为公共根基，超越一时一地的人事变迁。城邑迁徙，世局更替，但人类对滋养、秩序与共享资源的需求并未改变，所以“无丧无得”，井本身不因外在繁华而增减其价值。《彖传》说：“巽乎水而上水，井；井养而不穷也。改邑不改井，乃以刚中也。汔至亦未繘井，未有功也。羸其瓶，是以凶也。”这里点明井卦的关键不在“有资源”而在“能否成用”。即使来到井边，若不把水汲上来，仍然等于无功；若到了最后一刻反而打破水瓶，就会功亏一篑。故井卦告诫人们：根本条件再好，也必须经过整治、实践与器具配合，才能转化为现实利益。它反对空守资源、虚耗才德，更强调踏实经营、善用制度，使公共之利真正流通。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says, “The town may be changed, but the well cannot be changed. It neither loses nor gains. People come and go and draw from the well. If one almost reaches the water but does not lift it up, or breaks the jug, misfortune follows.” The point is that the well represents an enduring public foundation that remains necessary despite social change. External arrangements may shift, but the need for nourishment, order, and shared support remains constant. The Tuan Commentary explains: “Wood goes into the water and lifts the water upward: this is The Well. The well nourishes and does not run dry. ‘The town may be changed, but not the well’—this is because of firmness in the center. ‘Almost reaching it, yet not drawing from the well’ means no achievement has yet been made. ‘Breaking the jug’ is therefore misfortune.” This commentary makes clear that the hexagram is not satisfied with mere potential. A resource may exist, but unless it is properly accessed, preserved, and applied, it produces no real benefit. The Well teaches disciplined completion: cultivate the source, maintain the means, and carry usefulness through to the end.
imageDetailZh: 大象曰：“木上有水，井；君子以劳民劝相。”木上有水，是古人以木具引水上升之象，也象征深藏地下的资源经过合宜方式被提升出来，供众人使用。此处“劳民”不是苛役百姓，而是使民各安其业、共同修治公共之事；“劝相”则是鼓励互助合作，形成稳定的社会支持网络。井不是个人私享之物，而是需要共同维护、共同受益的公共设施。就人生而言，这一卦提示人应把自己的能力、知识与德行从潜藏状态中提炼出来，变成可被他人真正受用的东西；就治理而言，则强调基础设施、教育、制度与信任体系的建设。井之象贵在深、静、常、养，越是根本之物，越需要长期维护，而不是临渴掘井。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Wood above water: The Well. Thus the noble person encourages the people to work and exhorts mutual assistance.” The image comes from the ancient method of drawing water upward by means of wood. It suggests that what lies hidden below must be raised by proper means so that it can nourish the many. Here, “making the people work” does not mean burdening them harshly, but organizing useful effort around common needs; “encouraging mutual help” points to cooperation and shared maintenance. A well is never merely private wealth. It is a communal resource that requires care, order, and trust. Applied to human development, the image teaches that talent, wisdom, and virtue should not remain buried potential. They must be brought forth into practical usefulness. In social terms, it highlights the importance of institutions, education, infrastructure, and habits of reciprocity. The image values depth, quiet continuity, and patient maintenance over improvisation at the last minute.
modernApplicationZh: 事业：井卦主“基础价值”与“长期供给”。在职场中适合打磨专业能力、优化流程、建设团队知识库与稳定客户关系。短期不一定耀眼，却能成为组织离不开的核心支持者。若项目临门一脚却执行粗疏，便有“羸其瓶”之失。感情：此卦强调关系中的稳定供养，而非激情起伏。适合通过日常关怀、可靠承诺、持续沟通来建立信任；若一方长期封闭、积怨如井泥，则关系虽在却难以滋养彼此。财运：井卦不主暴利，利于经营长期资产、稳健现金流、可重复利用的资源与口碑型收益。财务上宜修补漏洞、整顿浪费，避免因工具失当或管理疏漏导致资源白白流失。健康：重点在调养与基础恢复，尤其关注水液代谢、肾气、脾胃与慢性疲劳问题。宜建立稳定作息、饮食与运动习惯，不宜等到明显透支才仓促补救。总体看，井卦提示现代人：真正的竞争力不只是爆发力，而是能否持续供给价值、被人信赖、在关键时刻真正派上用场。
modernApplicationEn: Career: The Well concerns foundational value and long-term usefulness. In work, it favors deepening professional skill, improving systems, building reliable knowledge bases, and maintaining steady relationships with clients or colleagues. Such efforts may not look dramatic in the short term, but they make a person indispensable over time. The warning is against failing at the final stage through sloppy execution—the classic image of breaking the jug just when the water is near. Relationships: This hexagram values steady nourishment over emotional spectacle. Trust grows through consistency, care, and dependable presence. If resentment, neglect, or emotional blockage accumulates like mud in a well, the relationship may still exist outwardly yet cease to nourish either person. Wealth: The Well is not a sign of quick windfalls. It supports long-term assets, recurring income, sustainable systems, and reputation-based gains. Financially, it advises repairing leaks, reducing waste, and improving the tools or processes through which value is retained. Health: The emphasis is on restoration, routine, and basic vitality rather than emergency fixes. Attention should be given to hydration, metabolism, kidney energy in traditional terms, digestion, and chronic fatigue. Overall, The Well teaches modern people that true strength lies not in occasional bursts, but in becoming a reliable source of value that can sustain life, work, and community over time.
historicalStoryZh: 可借汉代循吏治水修井的故事来理解井卦精神。古代地方官到任后，若见民间井泉淤塞、沟渠失修，往往不急于夸示政绩，而先整顿水利、修井通渠。百姓最初未必察觉其重要，待旱岁来临，方知一井之功，胜过空言百策。井卦所重，正是这种“不显而久利”的治理智慧：真正造福百姓的，不一定是轰动一时的举措，而是那些平日默默维持社会生机的基础工程。对个人也是如此，有德有才而不修、不用，如清泉埋于废井；能持久整理自己、磨炼器用、惠及他人，方能使潜德化为实福。
historicalStoryEn: A useful historical parallel for The Well is the conduct of conscientious local officials in early imperial China who repaired wells and irrigation before seeking visible glory. When entering office, the wisest among them did not rush to produce flashy achievements. Instead, they restored clogged wells, dredged channels, and secured the basic water supply of the people. At first, such work could seem plain or unimpressive. But when drought or hardship came, everyone understood that one functioning well was worth more than a hundred grand speeches. This reflects the spirit of Hexagram 48: the most valuable service is often quiet, foundational, and enduring. The same lesson applies personally. Talent and virtue that are left unused resemble clear water buried in an abandoned well. Only through cultivation, proper means, and service to others can hidden potential become actual blessing.
relatedHexagramsNote: 井卦与第四十八卦前后的困、革关系密切：困极则思求源，革后须立常法，故终归于井。又与水风井的对照卦火风鼎相参：鼎主变革烹饪、成文化之器，井主根基滋养、成生民之用，一动一常，共同说明文明既需更新，也需守本。
references: 《彖传》：巽乎水而上水，井；井养而不穷也。改邑不改井，乃以刚中也。汔至亦未繘井，未有功也。羸其瓶，是以凶也。
《象传》：木上有水，井；君子以劳民劝相。
